name: Palencian Romanesque Trail
name_original: Embalse de Aguilar – circular desde Aguilar de Campo
description: Cycle a challenging 13.7-mile (22.1 km) circular route around Aguilar Reservoir, featuring scenic views and demanding terrain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Aguilar De Campoo offers diverse landscapes, from the gentle hills and valleys of the Palentina Mountain foothills to the dramatic karst formations of the Las Tuerces Natural Area. The region is characterized by a mix of well-maintained secondary roads, scenic reservoir routes, and paths alongside the River Pisuerga. This area provides a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage, with routes often passing by historical Romanesque sites. Best touring cycling routes around Aguilar De Campoo The most popular touring cycling route is Palencian Romanesque Trail, a 13.7 miles (22.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route showcases the rich Romanesque heritage of the area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Collegiate Church of San Miguel – Pisuerga River loop from Aguilar de Campoo, a difficult 35.8 miles (57.6 km) path. This route follows the Pisuerga River and passes by the historic Collegiate Church of San Miguel. Local touring cyclists also love the Collegiate Church of St. Michael – Aguilar Reservoir loop from Aguilar de Campoo, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through the scenic Aguilar Reservoir area, often completed in about 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Aguilar De Campoo is defined by the Aguilar Reservoir, the unique karst formations of Las Tuerces Natural Area, and the gentle hills and steep climbs of the Palentina Mountain region. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging rides. The routes in Aguilar De Campoo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 180 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aguilar De Campoo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: Aguilar de Campoo offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 20 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy loops to more challenging rides, allowing you to explore the diverse landscapes of the region.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: The routes in Aguilar de Campoo vary in difficulty. You'll find 3 easy routes perfect for a relaxed ride, 7 moderate routes for those looking for a bit more challenge, and 14 difficult routes for experienced cyclists seeking significant elevation gains and longer distances.

Q: What historical and natural landmarks can I see while cycling in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: Many cycling routes in Aguilar de Campoo are designed to showcase the region's rich heritage and natural beauty. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María la Real Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, a significant Romanesque building, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4381459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CastroAmaya&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also feature natural wonders like the unique karst formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa&lt;/a&gt; within the Las Tuerces Natural Area, and scenic views of the Aguilar Reservoir.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Aguilar de Campoo has several easy touring cycling routes. A great option for beginners or families is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31878271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of St. Michael – Aguilar Reservoir loop from Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt;. This 6.3-mile (10.2 km) route takes you through the scenic Aguilar Reservoir area and is often completed in about 46 minutes.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists, there are 14 difficult routes available. One such challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of San Miguel – Pisuerga River loop from Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt;, a 35.8-mile (57.6 km) path that follows the Pisuerga River and passes by the historic Collegiate Church of San Miguel, offering significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about cycling in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: The touring cycling experience in Aguilar de Campoo is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the gentle hills to the dramatic karst formations, and the blend of natural beauty with rich cultural heritage found along the routes. Many appreciate the well-maintained secondary roads and the scenic reservoir views.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Aguilar de Campoo are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31884669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of St. Michael – Aguilar de Campoo Castle loop from Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 38.7-mile (62.3 km) circular route that offers historical sights and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Aguilar de Campoo?
A: Aguilar de Campoo benefits from moderate temperatures for much of the year, making it an appealing destination for touring cycling. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable weather conditions, with pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, ideal for exploring the region's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that focus on the Romanesque heritage of the area?
A: Yes, the region is known as the 'capital of the Palencia Romanesque,' and several routes highlight this rich heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170933618/palencian-romanesque-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencian Romanesque Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, showcasing numerous ancient churches and monasteries along its 13.7-mile (22.1 km) path.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views or viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain and prominent features like the Aguilar Reservoir provide numerous opportunities for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4459972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cross of Mount Bernorio&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3932858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Corbera&lt;/a&gt; are notable viewpoints. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4381408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar de Campoo Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched above the town, offers natural viewpoints over the surrounding area.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that follow rivers or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the Aguilar Reservoir is a focal point for several cycling routes, offering scenic views. The River Pisuerga also flows through the historic center of Aguilar de Campoo, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of St. Michael – Collegiate Church of San Miguel loop from Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt; incorporate sections along the river, adding to the picturesque setting.
tips: Ruins of the Castillo de Aguilar located on top of a strategic hill, watching over the beautiful biscuit town. The keep dates from the 9th and 10th centuries, while most of the walls are from the 14th and 15th centuries. Next to the Romanesque church of Santa Cecilia, on the same hill, it creates an impressive photographic image.
Section of gravel track with small ups and downs and a nice view with the mountains in the distance.

The Aguilar reservoir, inaugurated in 1964, plays a crucial role in regulating the Pisuerga River. With a height of 48 meters and a length of half a kilometer, the dam is one of the largest in the area. It is part of a set of three reservoirs and is considered a tourist attraction thanks to the "Ruta de los Pantanos".

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Aguilar Reservoir circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Aguilar de Campoo. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient base for accessing the reservoir and its surrounding trails. Specific parking areas are usually available near the town center or close to the reservoir's access points.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as difficult and requires good fitness. It's classified as a 'hard bike ride' due to varied and potentially demanding terrain, including sections where you may need to push your bike. Beginners might find it too challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route around Aguilar Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The route features varied and potentially demanding terrain. While it includes both paved and unpaved sections, the 'hard' rating suggests the presence of rougher paths and technical segments. Cyclists often use gravel bikes in the broader Aguilar de Campoo area, indicating that parts of the trail could be suitable for gravel riding, but be prepared for sections that may require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary draw is the continuous panoramic scenery of the Aguilar Reservoir. You'll also get Great views of Picos and can see the impressive dam, which is 48 meters high and half a kilometer long. Watching sunsets around the reservoir is highly recommended for its beautiful views.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The area around the reservoir is a vibrant recreational hub, especially during summer, offering opportunities for water sports and a swimming area. However, for cycling, spring and autumn generally offer more pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, but the scenic sunsets are a highlight.

faqs.question: Is the Aguilar Reservoir circular route dog-friendly for cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the general area is popular for outdoor activities, specific rules for dogs accompanying cyclists on this particular route are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas, and be mindful of other trail users and potential wildlife. Check local regulations for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route around the Aguilar Reservoir. It is generally open for public access.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs along the route or nearby?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Aguilar de Campoo, a town rich in historical and artistic heritage. You'll find various amenities, including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options, within the town itself. The area around the reservoir also offers recreational facilities, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with several other official trails. Approximately 50% of the route follows the Románico Palentino trail, and about 30% coincides with the Camino Olvidado (Bimón-Matamorisca). Smaller sections also intersect with the Camino del Besaya: Palencia, Travesía Marqués Aguilar, and Camino Fuente Quintana.

faqs.question: What makes this route challenging for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is considered challenging for touring cyclists due to its 'hard bike ride' classification, varied terrain, and the need for 'good fitness.' The presence of unpaved and potentially technical sections means you might need to push your bike for some segments, especially if you have a heavily loaded touring bicycle.

faqs.question: What cultural or historical sites are near the cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the cycling route itself focuses on the reservoir, it is situated in Aguilar de Campoo, known as the capital of Palencia Romanesque. Nearby attractions include the Monastery of Santa María la Real, the Collegiate Church of San Miguel, and the Hermitage of Santa Cecilia. You can also visit the Aguilar de Campoo Castle.
